political.s: Smithfield lean right politically, with FEC data showing $20K raised and 68% Republican donations (4250/6250). The company spent approximately $1.58M on lobbying in 2024-2025 and over $90K in political contributions during the 2024 cycle, focusing on agricultural and farming issues including efforts to limit animal welfare regulations.

charity.s: Smithfield demonstrates strong charitable giving, particularly in hunger relief. The company donated over 16 million protein servings valued at $18M+ in 2025, exceeding its 200-million-serving goal by 2025. Major focus areas include food insecurity, education, and minority farmer support through $15M Unity & Action program.
charity.focus: Hunger relief, education, minority farmer support, community development

environment.s: Mixed environmental record: Achieved 57% food waste reduction and 85% recyclable packaging materials, partnering with EPA on SmartWay logistics. However, listed as third-largest water polluter in U.S. with 66 environmental violations in 2019; faces ongoing EPA investigation into biogas operations affecting minority communities.

labor.s: Poor labor practices documented. Multiple NLRB cases involving unfair labor practices and worker organizing. Company used RICO laws against unionization efforts at Tar Heel facility. History of worker safety concerns, particularly during COVID-19 pandemic with inadequate PPE and slow response at Sioux Falls facility.

dei.s: Pro-DEI stance with $15M Unity & Action investment supporting minority farmers, HBCUs, and diverse hiring. Goals to increase racial diversity of leadership to 30% and gender diversity to 35% by 2030. Received NAMI honorable mention for DEI programs and increased minority-owned business spending by 14% by 2025.

animals.s: Tests on animals through standard agricultural production practices including genetic manipulation of pigs. Company claims ISO 34700 animal welfare certification but criticized by PETA for gestation crates, tail docking, teeth clipping without anesthesia, and reversal of prior animal welfare commitments. CEO announced 2007 phase-out of gestation crates but delayed indefinitely.

privacy.s: Limited public information on data privacy practices. As meat processing company, handles employee and potentially customer data. Recent IPO filing requirements (January 2025) mean increased disclosure of business data but privacy policies not prominently detailed.

execPay.s: CEO Shane Smith earned $14.9M in 2024 compensation, approximately 410x average worker salary (~$36.5K). Pay heavily weighted toward performance-based bonuses (89.9% non-salary). Compensation significantly exceeds peer companies like Hormel ($8.1M) and industry norms.

fec.committeeName: SMITHFIELD FOODS INC POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE, (HAMPAC)
